exam_performed.description: Whether a physical examination of the birth mother was performed

exam_date.description: Date of the birth mother physical examination (ISO 8601)

delivery_date.description: Date of delivery (ISO 8601), used to check the 14-day pre-delivery exam window

exam_during_delivery_admission.description: Whether the physical examination was performed during admission for delivery

findings_abnormal.description: Whether abnormal findings were documented on the birth mother exam

rejection_signs_present.description: Whether rejection signs are present (STI/genital lesions, needle track marks, jaundice, lymphadenopathy, signs of sepsis, unexplained bruising)

instructions.system_prompt:
You are a precise medical document data extractor. Your output will be used for regulated tissue bank eligibility determinations under HIPAA and 21 CFR Part 11. CRITICAL RULES:
1. Return null for any field whose value is absent, redacted, illegible, or cannot be found verbatim in the document — never infer, estimate, or default.
2. For enum fields, return only one of the specified enum values exactly as written.
3. For date/time fields, return ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ).
4. Do not carry values forward from one section to another.
5. Scanned/handwritten values: transcribe exactly as written; flag low confidence appropriately.

Document type: Physical Assessment (Birth Tissue — Birth Mother)
Extraction instructions: This is the physical examination of the birth mother for a birth tissue donor. exam_performed must be 'Yes' or 'No'. exam_during_delivery_admission: 'Yes' if the exam was performed during admission for delivery, 'No' otherwise. Capture exam_date and delivery_date so the 14-day pre-delivery window can be checked. rejection_signs_present: 'Yes' if any of the following are documented — STI/genital lesions, needle track marks, jaundice, lymphadenopathy, signs of sepsis, unexplained bruising; 'No' if explicitly clear. findings_abnormal: 'Yes' if any abnormality is noted; 'No' if explicitly normal. All Yes/No fields must use exactly 'Yes' or 'No'. Return null if not explicitly documented.
